front: What is the Bolzano-Weierstrass property in a metric space?
back: A metric space (X, d) has the Bolzano-Weierstrass property if every infinite subset of X has a limit point in X.

front: How is the Bolzano-Weierstrass property related to compactness?
back: For a metric space, compactness is equivalent to the Bolzano-Weierstrass property. This is a key theorem that connects the two concepts.

front: What is the classical Bolzano-Weierstrass theorem for ℝⁿ?
back: Every bounded sequence in ℝⁿ has a convergent subsequence. This is the special case of the general property applied to Euclidean space.

front: True or False: The set of all natural numbers ℕ with the discrete metric has the Bolzano-Weierstrass property.
back: False. In the discrete metric, no infinite subset has a limit point (since every singleton set is open), so the property fails.

front: What is the difference between 'limit point compact' and 'sequentially compact'?
back: In metric spaces, these are equivalent. A space is 'limit point compact' (has the Bolzano-Weierstrass property) iff it is 'sequentially compact' (every sequence has a convergent subsequence).
